title: App Install Retry Loop
description: Detects when the same app installation is attempted 3 or more times during one enrollment — a retry loop caused by unhonored reboot requests, repeated failures, or detection mismatches.
explanation:
The same app was started 3 or more times during this enrollment. Repeated install attempts have several possible causes:

- **Reboot-required loop** — the app requests a reboot (exit code 3010/1641) that the ESP does not honor, so the install-detect-retry cycle repeats
- **Failure retries** — the install keeps failing and the IME retries it
- **Detection mismatch** — the install succeeds but the detection rule does not match, so the app is retried as 'not installed'

Check the app's install results in this session to see which pattern applies; enrollments that legitimately span multiple reboots can also produce additional install starts.

remediation.title: Identify the retry cause
steps: Review this app's install started/completed/failed events in the session timeline
If reboot-related: check the app's return code mapping includes reboot codes (3010, 1641) and verify ESP reboot behavior settings; consider the 'Hard reboot' return code in Intune
If the install keeps failing: look up the failure error code (see the App Installation Error finding if it fired)
If the install succeeds but is retried: fix the app's detection rule (see the Win32 App Detection Script Failure finding)
